headline: School politics class: Labour sheds support from impatient Britons
body_text:
This article picked by a teacher with suggested questions is part of the Financial Times free schools access programme. Details/registration here.

Read our full range of politics picks here.

Both examination boards require you to study the factors that influence voting behaviour. Perceptions of governing competence, and the so-called ‘feel-good’ (or ‘feel-bad’) factor are important considerations for many voters. This is particularly true in an era of ‘partisan dealignment’, when traditional party loyalties are weaker than they once were.

The article discusses how long voters are willing to wait for a government to carry out its promises to improve the economy, public services and living standards more generally. It quotes a survey which notes an interesting possible gender divide on this issue.

Click the link below to read the article and then answer the questions:

Labour sheds support from impatient Britons

Question in the style of AQA Politics Paper 1
